title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Judge Bars ICE From Making Immigration Arrests at Courts in New York
description: The Trump administration’s practice of detaining migrants at federal courthouses had stirred outrage.
author: Jonah E. Bromwich and Benjamin Weiser

title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
description: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power
author: Lydia Polgreen

title: Trump’s Deportations Are Costing Americans Jobs, Study Finds
description: According to a new study, construction was impacted more than any other industry studied, with American-born workers losing more jobs than immigrants as a result of the deportations.
author: Ronda Kaysen

title: Why College Grads Are Booing Their Commencement Speakers
description: The generation that grew up with A.I. hates it.
author: Michelle Goldberg

title: Activity Slows in Sandy Fire in Simi Valley. Evacuation Orders Remain.
description: More than 33,000 people remain under evacuation orders after a brush fire broke out in Simi Valley, Calif.
author: Soumya Karlamangla and Amy Graff

title: The Pressing Case for Supreme Court Reform
description: In a representative democracy, no one should hold so much power for so long.
author: Jesse Wegman

title: Ebola Outbreak in Central Africa Could Last Months, W.H.O. Says
description: Health officials reported more than 130 suspected deaths and 513 cases, a sharp rise since the outbreak was identified on May 15 in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.
author: Lynsey Chutel and Yan Zhuang

title: E.P.A. to Repeal Some Limits on ‘Forever Chemicals’ in Drinking Water
description: The rules were established by the Biden administration after research linked the compounds to a range of serious health problems.
author: Lisa Friedman

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak as Cases and Deaths Rise
description: More than 130 deaths and over 500 cases are suspected. The United States restricted entry for people who have been to three African countries after a global health emergency was declared.
author: Ephrat Livni

title: Oil Slick Reaches a Pristine Persian Gulf Island in Iran
description: Videos show birds, turtles and crabs trapped inside mounds of tar around Shidvar island, a protected wildlife sanctuary with turquoise waters and white sand beaches.
author: Sanam Mahoozi, Erika Solomon and Devon Lum

title: Top Treasury Lawyer Resigns After Creation of ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’
description: Brian Morrissey, the department’s general counsel, stepped down hours after the Trump administration announced the $1.8 billion fund.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
description: The Trump administration argues that an “emergency refugee situation” in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at a cost of some $100 million.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz

title: Brookings Institution Report: Over 100,000 Family Separations in Trump Crackdown
description: The Brookings Institution suggests that federal statistics are an undercount because immigrant parents are not being asked about or not disclosing their American children.
author: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson

title: Trump Threatens Iran and Then Pulls Back, All in the Same Day
description: President Trump has repeatedly said he’ll restart military action against Iran, only to stop short of plunging the United States directly back into an unpopular war.
author: Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt

title: Justice Dept. Sets Up $1.8 Billion Fund That Could Funnel Money to Trump Allies
description: The arrangement was denounced by critics as a slush fund for supporters of President Trump, possibly including Jan. 6 rioters.
author: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
description: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
author: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith

title: N.Y.C. Hotel Housekeepers Will Earn Over $100,000 Under New Contract
description: A deal between a powerful union and an industry group substantially boosts annual pay for workers coping with New York City’s high cost of living.
author: Patrick McGeehan

title: Mark Fuhrman, Flawed Witness in O.J. Simpson Trial, Dies at 74
description: A Los Angeles police detective, he was discredited during Mr. Simpson’s 1995 murder trial by defense lawyers who pointed to his past use of racist language.
author: Clyde Haberman

title: American Man Tests Positive for Ebola Amid Outbreak, Officials Say
description: An American doctor working with a Christian missionary organization in the Democratic Republic of Congo was exposed to the deadly virus while treating patients, the organization said.
author: Yan Zhuang and Hannah Ziegler
